mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic health, which translates into higher corporate earnings and thus positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening to curb price pressures, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average during periods where both CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion indicate strong investment and consumer confidence, boosting overall economic activity and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive HOUST growth during economic expansions does not correlate with positive S&P 500 20-day returns, or if the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat on average when INDPRO shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) and a stable or declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a strong and tightening labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE 3-month change < 0.1%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of strong payroll growth combined with stable or falling unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: A significant drop in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive easing by the central bank, aiming to stimulate economic activity by lowering borrowing costs, which typically boosts equity valuations and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances where FEDFUNDS drops by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a favorable environment for corporate earnings and thus drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average during periods of positive GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth.
